document_index.excerpt: Intro tweet: On #WorldCancerResearchDay, we’re reflecting on why we dedicate our work to cancer research👩🔬🎗️👨🔬. Stay tuned as some of our lab members share what motivates them to make a difference in this field🌍💙 #wecan #workingtogether #collaboration Tweet 2: Here’s why @afarkkila, our PI, is committed to cancer research: "With our truly translational setting and interdisciplinary team, we are in such a unique position to impact patients’ lives." #WorldCancerResearchDay Reply to tweet 2: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke Tweet 3: Here’s why Anna Nylund from our lab is committed to cancer research: Cancer is one of the leading causes of death, and I’m highly motivated to find answers that can save more lives. #WorldCan

The app is available in English. Then select as in the picture on the left. <-Here, selected are AB travel zone (which covers all Helsinki), adult as customer type, that ticket starts immediately and that its duration is 4 days. If buying a ticket on Friday evening: remember to select validity starts only on Saturday morning to be able to use the ticket for Tuesday as well! We hope you find Helsinki pleasant and enjoy your holiday here! vector_chunks.chunk_id: Lab parties /Halloween 2021/2021_Halloween party_planning.docx::chunk_0000
vector_chunks.source_file: Lab parties /Halloween 2021/2021_Halloween party_planning.docx
vector_chunks.text:
Halloween party
Official notes
Estimation of participants = 40
Drinks
Soft Drinks (Karen)
Beer, Cider (Karen)
Wine (Karen)
Tequila (Färkkilä lab; ask from Karen)
Food
Pizza (Färkkilä lab)
Pizzarium (Fernando will decide which ones to order).
When pizzas arrive please put clear labels for which pizza is which.
Arrange beforehand a space where the pizzas will be.
Snacks: popcorn, candy, chips (Karen)
A chocolate box for the prize of the scary tweet contest (Karen)
Costumes
What do we wear
What are we offering
Facepaint (Julia brings from home)
Stuff from Tiger (Elina will check)
Decoration
Spider web (Nastya checks)
Table cloth (Nastya checks)
Christmas lights
Julia brings x1 from home
Elina brings x1 from home
Inga-Maria brings x1 from home
Everyone else who has one, please bring
Balloons (Inga-Maria / Elina checks)
Other consumables (Inga-Maria / Elina checks)
Plates
Cups
Altar of the dead (Anniina and Fernando)
Program items
Advertisement of the program (Sagarika)
Competitions 
 Best costume
Price – tequila bottle (Ask from Karen)
Everyone votes during the evening – announced at 8 pm
Voting on the white board
Kick-off game
Dividing participants at the start
Vampires / Witches / Spiders / Bats /Werewolves / Mummies (print-outs: Julia)
Pumpkin decoration (6x pumpkins: Anniina)
The scariest tweet 
Specific #’s: #oncosys # (ask from Karen)
Evaluation: the more retweets and or likes after 2 hours wins
Price – chocolate to share within the group (chocolate: Karen)
Program:
16:00 Introduce the program:
 The team tags and Pumpkin & Scary tweet contest -> prize
 Halloween costume contest -> prize
17:00 Pizzas should arrive
18:00 Prize giving ceremony:
 - for tweet with most likes/retweets the chocolate box
 - tequila bottle for best costume

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Outreach_and_social_media/World_cancer_research_day_2024.docx::chunk_0000
vector_chunks.source_file: Outreach_and_social_media/World_cancer_research_day_2024.docx
vector_chunks.text:
Intro tweet: On #WorldCancerResearchDay, we’re reflecting on why we dedicate our work to cancer research👩🔬🎗️👨🔬. Stay tuned as some of our lab members share what motivates them to make a difference in this field🌍💙 #wecan #workingtogether #collaboration 
Tweet 2: Here’s why @afarkkila, our PI, is committed to cancer research: "With our truly translational setting and interdisciplinary team, we are in such a unique position to impact patients’ lives." #WorldCancerResearchDay 
Reply to tweet 2: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke
Tweet 3: Here’s why Anna Nylund from our lab is committed to cancer research: Cancer is one of the leading causes of death, and I’m highly motivated to find answers that can save more lives. #WorldCancerResearchDay
Reply to tweet 3: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke @WCRD24Sept 
Tweet 4: Here’s why Aino Elomaa from our lab is committed to cancer research: "I want to work on something that feels meaningful, and with cancer being such a common disease, it’s a way to make a real difference." #WorldCancerResearchDay
Reply to Tweet 4: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke @WCRD24Sept 
Tweet 5: Here’s why @adajnq from our lab is committed to cancer research: "Cancer is not just a disease, it’s a lens through which we can uncover the mysteries of life at its core. I want to shape a better future for patients." #WorldCancerResearchDay
Reply to Tweet 5: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke @WCRD24Sept

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Outreach_and_social_media/World_cancer_research_day_2024.docx::chunk_0001
vector_chunks.source_file: Outreach_and_social_media/World_cancer_research_day_2024.docx
vector_chunks.text:
ncover the mysteries of life at its core. I want to shape a better future for patients." #WorldCancerResearchDay
Reply to Tweet 5: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke @WCRD24Sept 
Tweet 6: Here’s why @MariaMHincapie from our lab is committed to cancer research: "Cancer still claims too many lives, and I want to use my skills to change that. That’s why I’m dedicated to advancing cancer research and improving outcomes for patients." #WorldCancerResearchDay
Reply to Tweet 5: @Syopapotilaat @Syopajarjestot @gysysuomi @HUS_fi @HelsinkiUniMed @iCAN_Finland @FIMM_UH @LiljestromMarke @WCRD24Sept 
Tweet 7: Wrapping up #WorldCancerResearchDay with some exciting news—our latest paper has been accepted for publication! 🎉 We’re celebrating with cake, sparkling wine, and renewed energy to keep pushing forward in the fight against cancer. 🍰🥂 #wecan #workingtogether 
Reply to Tweet 7:

vector_chunks.text:
Schedule for Prof Iain McNeish’s visit to University of Helsinki 
Hosts: 
Fernando Perez +358 44 9264369
Anniina Färkkilä +358 50 5967786
Joonas Jukonen +358 50 5748092
Wed May 7th
Arrival AY1338 18:10 LHR - 23:00 HEL
Hotel Scandic Park, Mannerheimintie 46, 00260 Helsinki
(https://maps.app.goo.gl/TDYcVVCDX6Le5Zh89) 
Thursday May 8th
Biomedicum Helsinki, Haartmaninkatu 8
https://maps.app.goo.gl/xcL8CfpM7eSF3fx79
11:30-12:00 Welcome to our institute and laboratory
12:00-13:00 Student lunch hosted by Fernando
13:00-14.00 Meeting with Liisa Kauppi
14:00-15.00 Meeting with Anniina
15:10-15:30 Gathering before your seminar with coffee and bun
15:30-16:30 Your talk as part of the iCAN seminars 
16:30-17:30 Meeting with Anna Vähärautio
19.00-22:00 Dinner with Anniina, Liisa, Anna, and Jaana Oikkonen
At Ravintola Nokka, Kanavanranta 7 F, 00160 Helsinki
(https://nokkahelsinki.fi/, https://maps.app.goo.gl/kaYHawexDLfiucNv6)
Friday May 9th
Biomedicum Helsinki, Haartmaninkatu 8
https://maps.app.goo.gl/xcL8CfpM7eSF3fx79
11:00-11.30 Meeting with Anniina and Fernando on Finnish defense protocol
11:30-12.30 Lunch with Anniina and researchers
12.30-13:00 Gathering at Liisa’s office in preparation of the thesis defense
13:00-15:30 Fernando's doctoral thesis defense 
15:30-16:30 Toast and small gathering after defense
16:30-17:30 Break
18:00-Late night Fernando's Karonkka, formal dinner and celebration in your honor
At Teatterimuseo, Tallberginkatu 1, 00180 Helsinki
(https://www.teatterimuseo.fi/, https://maps.app.goo.gl/z24WMimmgL2s7fXc8) 
Saturday May 10th
Departure AY1331 08:00 HEL - 09:10 LHR

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app.docx::chunk_0000
vector_chunks.source_file: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app.docx
vector_chunks.text:
For your stay, we recommend a 4 day ticket as it is the most convenient. 4-day-ticket is 20€ per adult and paid in the app from the debit/card.
The app is available in English. If app downloads in Finnish, you can easily change settings from “Lisää” next to three lines-icon, “Asetukset”->”Kieli”->English
How to buy 4-day-ticket: 
Tickets->Day tickets. Then select as in the picture on the left.
<-Here, selected are AB travel zone (which covers all Helsinki), adult as customer type, that ticket starts immediately and that its duration is 4 days. 
If buying a ticket on Friday evening: remember to select validity starts only on Saturday morning to be able to use the ticket for Tuesday as well!
For payment method you have to select “Add payment card” to be able to add your card details. 
Notice: app moves you to separate (Finnish) site to fill in details, however it is safe to use it as this part of public service (substantial part of public transport is tax-funded in Finland)
The page for adding card details is in Finnish, add card number (kortin numero), date and year of expiration (kortti voimassa, kk and vv) and safecode CVC. 
When details are correct, press “Jatka” (continue) to get back to the app. Now you can buy the 4-day-ticket =) In the buying process one needs to be patient and whenever one sees any loading-circles, do not close the app.
When your ticket is valid, the ticket-page shows a moving icon with changing colour background + QR-code on Tickets-page and ticket details-
Notice: Every traveler needs an own ticket, perhaps the handiest way is to download their own app for everyone.

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app.docx::chunk_0001
vector_chunks.source_file: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app.docx
vector_chunks.text:
en your ticket is valid, the ticket-page shows a moving icon with changing colour background + QR-code on Tickets-page and ticket details-
Notice: Every traveler needs an own ticket, perhaps the handiest way is to download their own app for everyone. Tickets need to be shown to drivers in buses (except orange ones) and to be kept available (be sure to load your smartphone, app consumes relatively much battery) whenever using transports as ticket inspection is possible.
TIPS: When discovering the town on your own you can search public transportation options from “Routes” by adding start and destination + desired time. After that, a list of options appears, and from Settings of that page more criteria can be filled in.

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx::chunk_0000
vector_chunks.source_file: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx
vector_chunks.text:
Welcome to Helsinki Alan and Bonnie!
In this info-package you can find a schedule of hosted events, responsibility persons and their contact-info and, in addition, some tips for your trip. We hope you find Helsinki pleasant and enjoy your holiday here!
Friday evening / Saturday morning 
Before leaving the hotel or first thing when arriving at Kauppatori if not using public transport to get there - Elina is glad to help then if needed!
You might like to consider downloading app for buying public transportation (HSL) tickets and for seeing public transportation routes: 
->for Apple HSL App Storessa 
->for Android HSL - tickets & route planner - Apps on Google Play 
For your stay, we recommend a 4 day ticket as it is the most convenient. 4-day-ticket is 20€ per adult and paid in the app from the debit/card.
The app is available in English. If app downloads in Finnish, you can easily change settings from “Lisää” next to three lines-icon, “Asetukset”->”Kieli”->English
See instructions for buying the tickets here, click: Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app 
Saturday 2.7. Trip to Suomenlinna 

Responsible persons: Alfredo, Fernando and Elina (+358407494247)
UPDATE: Alfredo, Fernando and Elina will pick you up from your hotel 11.30
Meeting point: Kauppatori, Eteläranta, 00170 Helsinki in front of famous Havis Amanda statue. Click here to see location: Havis Amanda 
TIP: we recommend to take a route to Kauppatori such that you can walk along Bulevardi and Esplanadin puisto (Esplanadi park), click: Recommended route to walk to Kauppatori 
At Kauppatori, souvenirs, morning coffee, and ice cream etc. are available. Be aware of seagulls if eating something.
Boat 19 to Suomenlinna departs from Kauppatori (see map below) 
Departure times are 20 and 50 past hour (i.g.

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx::chunk_0001
vector_chunks.source_file: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx
vector_chunks.text:
to walk to Kauppatori 
At Kauppatori, souvenirs, morning coffee, and ice cream etc. are available. Be aware of seagulls if eating something.
Boat 19 to Suomenlinna departs from Kauppatori (see map below) 
Departure times are 20 and 50 past hour (i.g. 8:20 and 8:50) (click here for full boat schedule: Linja 19) Boat trip takes around 15 minutes. 
HSL day ticket AB covers that trip, tickets are shown to a machine in the terminal before entering the boat.
Suomenlinna is an important historical island at the front of Helsinki that has a lot to offer. There is a port, stony fortitude and several restaurants. Here from official website can be read more of Suomenlinna: Suomenlinna (in English)
They have a guided tour at the fortitude to discover the area's history in English 13:30 from Suomenlinnakeskus (address: Suomenlinna C 74, 00190 Helsinki), its duration approx. 1h. Tickets to guided tour for adults 12€/person, reserved from this page: Suomenlinna tours reservation 
Suomenlinna has several suitable restaurants, open as follows:
Locations and more info can be found from, click: Suomenlinna page (in English). Some notes: Bastion Bistro is perhaps the finest of these, Valimo is more family-friendly but still nice looking, Deli&Cafe serves lunch and cafeteria food and Pizzeria Nikolai serves fine pizzas.
Sunday 3.7.
Anniina will take you to beautiful Porvoo - a city of wooden architecture, lovely cafés and boutiques! See more about Porvoo here, click: Visit Porvoo (English) 
Meeting point: Anniina picks you up with her car from your hotel around 10.
For the evening, Anniina has reserved a table from restaurant Saari 18:30, to where you arrive together by boat from Merisatamanranta 10, 00150 Helsinki. More info of restaurant, click: Ravintola Saari (English) 
4.7.

car from your hotel around 10.
For the evening, Anniina has reserved a table from restaurant Saari 18:30, to where you arrive together by boat from Merisatamanranta 10, 00150 Helsinki. More info of restaurant, click: Ravintola Saari (English) 
4.7.
Trip to Seurasaari + visit the Färkkilä lab and Meilahti campus
If you, Bonnie, don't want to join in for the afternoon in the lab (though we would be very happy to have you here!), there are several suggestions of great activities and places to visit at the end of this infopackage that you might then consider. Special recommendation for Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden + Cafe Viola from Suvi!
Tuesday 5.7. 
Helsinki-tour
Recommended activity for Tuesday evening to you two: 
Sauna at Löyly - visitor-friendly Sauna at Helsinki, amazing view and terrace
Address: Hernesaarenranta 4, 00150 Helsinki
More about Löyly, click Löyly (in English)
Sauna slots are 2h, one can stay up to that time. Please notice that Löyly requires using swimwear in sauna (rent possible, cost 6 euros per person)
Sauna entrance 21€ person (includes towel, pefletti* and soap). Booking is recommended to be done beforehand from here: Sauna reservation - Ravintola Löyly Helsinki Notice: Reservation has to be paid in 10 min after making it
They also have a restaurant at Löyly, however during summertime booking is not possible - first come first served
*pefletti is a small towel (paper or fabric made) on which one sits in sauna
Tips of great activities to do on your own at Helsinki if you have time&energy:
visit: 
National natural museum, open daily from Mon.­–Sun. from 10 am to 5 pm. Address: Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13, 00100 Helsinki. More info and prices: https://www.luomus.fi/en/natural-history-museum 
Kiasma (national museum of modern art).

vector_chunks.chunk_id: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx::chunk_0003
vector_chunks.source_file: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx
vector_chunks.text:
energy:
visit: 
National natural museum, open daily from Mon.­–Sun. from 10 am to 5 pm. Address: Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13, 00100 Helsinki. More info and prices: https://www.luomus.fi/en/natural-history-museum 
Kiasma (national museum of modern art). Address: Mannerheiminaukio 2, 00100 Helsinki. Schedule, info, prices and tickets: https://kiasma.fi/en/ Please notice that Kiasma (as many other museums) is closed on Mondays.
Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden: The outdoor garden, covering approximately four hectares, attracts the visitor to experience the world of plants. In summertime, perhaps the most attractive part of the grounds is the central garden, an open area with ponds and flowers, bordered by glasshouses (picture) and rose bushes. A historic stone building envisioned as the king’s castle now houses a herbarium in the middle of the garden. 
Address: Kaisaniemenranta 2
00170 Helsinki. Entrance to the area is free of charge, entrance price to greenhouses is 12€ for adults. Official web page, click Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden | LUOMUS 
Meilahti arboretum (flower park somewhat near Meilahti campus). Free of Charge. Address: Johannesbergintie 7, 00250 Helsinki
eat&drink: 
If you visit Kaisaniemen Kasvitieteellinen puutarha, be sure to eat in Cafe Viola (address Kaisaniemenranta 2, 00170 Helsinki). It is located right next to Kaisaniemen kasvitieteellinen puutarha and open 10-18 weekdays. The place is awesome, warm recommendation from Suvi who has visited it several times.
Kahvila Rakastan: fully plant-based, traditional Finnish kitchen inspired cafée: https://kahvilarakastan.fi/herkut/ Address: Mannerheimintie 13 B,
00100 Helsinki
Kiasma has also cafeteria: Kiasma Cafe serves freshly made lunch and cafeteria bites. (Mannerheiminaukio 2, 00100 Helsinki)

Cafe Engel is a historical place and very beautiful cafe in the heart of Helsinki, next to Senaatintori. Address: Aleksanterinkatu 26, 00170 Helsinki. Open from 8. https://www.cafeengel.fi/ 
Restaurant Sunn. Nordic kitchen, lovely view to Senaatintori and beautiful setting. Address: Aleksanterinkatu 26, 2.krs, 00170 Helsinki. https://ravintolasunn.fi/
We hope you will enjoy your trip to Helsinki! 
We are happy to answer any possible questions and help you, so please do not hesitate to ask us.
